# Cold Handoff — `openclown-assistant` Orchestrator → New Devin Account

**Generated:** 2026-04-30 23:25 UTC, auto-cold after the close of the **TKT-006** (Food Lookup KBJU Estimator) cycle + the carried-debt validator/reviewer.md cycle (PR #46) + the PR-Agent full pipeline integration cycle (PR #47).
**Type:** COLD (auto-generated, formal state only — no texture / no observations).
**Handoff target:** Fresh Devin session in any account that can be authenticated to the `OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant` repo.
**Outgoing session:** `https://app.devin.ai/sessions/2ff3c66567464a138ddb3be9e89a26a1` (continuation of `2026-04-30-session-2.md` after the ADR-010 cycle close).

---

## 0. What you (new Devin) are looking at

You are taking over the **AI-engineering mentor / orchestrator** role for `openclown-assistant`. This file is your full bootstrap. Read every section before touching the repo.

Your three core obligations (full prompt in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md`, read it after the self-checks in §1):

1. **Teach** the PO (non-engineer founder learning the SDLC).
2. **Push back** with evidence; cite artifacts and ADRs. Sycophancy is a failure mode.
3. **Protect docs-as-code:** every architectural decision must live in a versioned markdown file in git. "We agreed in chat" is not a decision.

You are NOT one of the four pipeline LLM agents (Business Planner, Architect, Executor, Reviewer). You are the conductor.

---

## 1. Self-check (run BEFORE asking the PO anything)

### 1a. `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN`

Call:

```
secrets action="list"
```

If you see `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN` (or fallback names like `GITHUB_PAT_TOKEN2`, `GITHUB_PAT`, `GH_TOKEN`) → OK, skip to 1b.

If none present, request via:

```
secrets action="request" secret_name="GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN" type="plain" should_save=true save_scope="user" note="GitHub fine-grained PAT for OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant: Contents R/W + Pull requests R/W + Workflows R/W"
```

Send a non-blocking message to the PO explaining what scopes the PAT needs.

**Note (delta from session-1):** in the outgoing session this secret was NOT present, but the Devin native git proxy (`git-manager.devin.ai/proxy/github.com/...`) authenticated `git fetch` / `git push` directly without it. The PAT is only needed if you must use `gh` CLI as a fallback OR if your account lacks the native git integration. The repo is public so read-only `git clone` works without any token.

### 1b. Repo clone

```bash
[[ -d ~/repos/openclown-assistant/.git ]] && echo "REPO_OK" || echo "REPO_MISSING"
```

If `REPO_OK` → `cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant && git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only origin main`.

If `REPO_MISSING`:

```bash
mkdir -p ~/repos
git clone https://github.com/OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant.git ~/repos/openclown-assistant
cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
```

(Native git proxy auto-rewrites `https://github.com/...` to `https://git-manager.devin.ai/proxy/github.com/...` for you.)

### 1c. Validator green on current main

```bash
cd ~/repos/openclown-assistant
python3 scripts/validate_docs.py | tail -3
```

Expect: `validated 46 artifact(s); 0 failed`.

If `pyyaml` is missing in the python env (the validator imports it), run `pip install pyyaml` once. The org-level env config (accepted 2026-04-30) already includes `pip install pyyaml` as `maintenance` — fresh sessions in subsequent snapshot builds should not need this.

If validator fails, **stop and tell the PO** — main should never be in a broken validator state.

### 1d. `gh` CLI (Devin's native tools usually replace it)

```bash
gh --version 2>/dev/null && gh auth status 2>/dev/null && echo "GH_OK" || echo "GH_MISSING_OR_UNAUTHED"
```

Use the native tools wherever possible: `git_view_pr` / `git_create_pr` / `git_pr_checks` / `git_ci_job_logs` / `git_comment_on_pr` / `git_update_pr_description`. They are faster and always authenticated.

## 3. Project context (stable — full version in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §2)

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Product | KBJU Coach v0.1 — Telegram bot for calorie/macro tracking |
| Target users | 2 pilots (PO + partner). Multi-tenant scoped from day 1 for future monetization. |
| Repo | `OpenClown-bot/openclown-assistant` (public) |
| Token name | `GITHUB_TOKEN_OPENCLOWN` (canonical; native git proxy works without it for most actions) |
| Production runtime | OpenClaw skill, TypeScript on Node 24, Docker Compose to a single VPS |
| LLM stack | OmniRoute → Fireworks pool. Architect: GPT-5.5 (xhigh) primary / Opus 4.7 thinking Windsurf alt / Opus 4.6 thinking opencode fallback. Executor: GLM 5.1 default; Qwen 3.6 Plus / Codex GPT-5.5 listed but unused in pilot. Reviewer: Kimi K2.6 (single source of truth, never varies). |
| Repo VPS | 6 vCPU / 7.6 GiB RAM / 75 GB / Ubuntu 24.04. Repo path on VPS: `~/repos/openclown-assistant`. |
| Validator | `python3 scripts/validate_docs.py` (`pyyaml` already in env config — `maintenance` section installs it on every snapshot build) |
| Pre-commit hooks | None at this snapshot — verify with `ls .pre-commit-config.yaml .husky/ 2>/dev/null` and act on actual repo state |
| Inline-AI PR review bot | **Qodo PR-Agent running Qwen 3.6 Plus via OmniRoute / Fireworks**, configured in `.github/workflows/pr_agent.yml` + `.pr_agent.toml`. Heavy-tuned 2026-04-30 via PR #47 (full pipeline integration): `num_max_findings=6`, `require_security_review`/`require_can_be_split_review`/`require_ticket_analysis_review`, `commitable_code_suggestions=true`, repo-aware `extra_instructions` for `[pr_reviewer]` + `[pr_description]` + `[pr_code_suggestions]` (encodes docs-as-code conventions, AC traceability, scope discipline, Cyrillic security focus, concurrency-safety patterns, TS idioms, test conventions). Triggers expanded to include `synchronize` (auto-rerun on iter-N pushes), `pull_request_review_comment` (reply to threads), `workflow_dispatch` (manual). `ignore_pr_title` filters skip `^clerical:`, `^session-log:`, `^Auto`, `^[Auto]` — confirmed working: PR-Agent skipped on PRs #45/#46/#47/#48. **Replaces** Devin Review (PO ran out of ACUs 2026-04-30 mid-session). PR-Agent is the **second** reviewer; Kimi K2.6 remains load-bearing for verdicts. |

## 4. Pipeline (stable)

```
PRD (Business Planner)
  └→ Reviewer #1 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Planner family)
       └→ ArchSpec + ADRs + Tickets (Architect)
            └→ Reviewer #2 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Architect family)
                 └→ Code (Executor; one Ticket per session; GLM/Qwen/Codex)
                      └→ Reviewer #3 (RV-CODE, Kimi K2.6) + Qodo PR-Agent inline bot
                           └→ PO merges
```

**Session independence.** Every artifact gets a new LLM session. No reuse across artifacts. **Exception:** Reviewer iter-N must reuse the same Kimi K2.6 opencode-session that produced iter-1 (continuity preserves context for re-eval).

**Branch hygiene for review PRs.** Reviewer sessions branch their review-PR **from `main`**, never from the artifact's own branch (per `docs/prompts/reviewer.md` §A.15 / §B.15). **Known footgun (carried debt, see §6.4 step 4):** when the target artifact is unmerged (e.g. SPEC review of a `proposed` ADR on its own arch-branch), the validator on the rv-branch fails because cross-references (`target_ref`) cannot resolve to a file on the from-`main` rv-branch tree. Workaround until the carried-debt PR lands: rebase the rv-branch onto the artifact's arch-branch BEFORE pushing the iter-N commit. RV-SPEC-005 iter-2 used exactly this workaround on 2026-04-30 (NUDGE-iter-2 instructed Kimi to `git rebase origin/arch/ADR-010-calorie-floor` before re-eval push); validator went from 19 errors → 44/0.

**Q-TKT trigger.** If the spec lacks a decisive answer, Executor MUST create `docs/questions/Q-TKT-<NNN>-<NN>.md` and stop. Do NOT guess. This is codified in `docs/prompts/executor.md`; precedent: Q-TKT-005-01 produced ADR-005@0.2.0 amendment cycle.

**NUDGE iteration model.** When Reviewer / PR-Agent surface findings, Executor (or Reviewer) addresses them in iter-N+1 on the same artifact/PR via continuation message into the existing opencode-session. Verdict scheme (`docs/prompts/reviewer.md` §A.14 / §B.14): `pass` (zero findings), `pass_with_changes` (zero HIGH findings; medium/low may remain deferred per PO), `fail` (≥1 HIGH finding).

---

## 5. Roles and write-zones (canonical: `CONTRIBUTING.md` lines 7-22)

| Role | Default model | Runtime | MAY write | MUST NOT write |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Product Owner (human) | — | — | Anything (final authority) | — |
| Business Planner | GPT-5.5 thinking / Opus 4.7 thinking | ChatGPT Plus / Devin | `docs/prd/` | Everything else |
| Architect | GPT-5.5 xhigh primary / Opus 4.7 thinking Windsurf alt | opencode CLI / Windsurf | `docs/architecture/`, `docs/tickets/` | `docs/prd/`, `src/`, `tests/`, `infra/`, repo root |
| Executor | GLM 5.1 default | opencode + OmniRoute | `src/`, `tests/`, append-only `docs/tickets/<id>.md#10 Execution Log`, ticket `status` (4 transitions only), `docs/questions/` | `docs/prd/`, `docs/architecture/`, any other ticket field, anything outside ticket §5 Outputs |
| Reviewer (LLM) | Kimi K2.6 | opencode + OmniRoute | `docs/reviews/` | Everything else; **NEVER `status: approved`** (PO-delegated orchestrator clerical patch sets that based on Reviewer verdict) |
| Reviewer (auto bot) | Qodo PR-Agent / Qwen 3.6 Plus via OmniRoute | GitHub Actions | inline PR comments + auto-generated PR descriptions (via `use_description_markers`) | files in repo |
| **Orchestrator (you)** | Devin | webapp | Coordination + `docs/session-log/` + `docs/backlog/` (light) + ticket frontmatter promotions (`status`, `arch_ref`, `version`, `updated`, plus established closure fields `closed_at`/`closed_by`/`closure_pr`/`closure_commit`/`review_ref` per TKT-001..005 precedent) + RV frontmatter promotion (`status: in_review` → `approved` + `approved_at`/`approved_by`/`approved_after_iters`/`approved_note`) + ADR frontmatter promotion (`status: proposed` → `accepted` + `review_refs`/`reviewer_models`/`approved_at`/`approved_by`/`approved_note` per ADR-005@0.2.0 + ADR-010@0.1.0 precedent) per `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §5 PO-delegated clerical patch carve-out | code, formal artifact bodies (PRD/ARCH/ADR/RV), substantive ticket body edits beyond reference-pinning, `docs/prompts/`, `CONTRIBUTING.md` |

**Orchestrator-write-zone hard rule (CONTRIBUTING.md line 22):** any orchestrator edit to files outside this declared write-zone (e.g. `CONTRIBUTING.md` itself, `docs/prompts/`, `scripts/`, formal artifact bodies) requires **explicit PO authorisation recorded verbatim in the PR body**. Without it Reviewer / PR-Agent will reject the PR. **Precedents (verbatim quotes captured in PR bodies):** PR #39 architect.md edit, PR #46 (`«Авторизация carried-debt PR - чини, если это нужно проекту»`), PR #47 (`«если как-то можешь улучшить конфиг pr-agenta - делай, нам нужен лучший второй ревьюер, выжми макс. и вообще полностью интегрируй полноценно пр-агента в наш пайплайн»`).

PO triggered `/improve` manually for dogfood test — workflow run completed `success` with no output (markdown-only diff, expected: PR-Agent's `patch_extension_skip_types` excludes `.md` for code suggestions).

13. **PR-Agent integration verified end-to-end.** GitHub Actions runs API confirmed: 10 total runs, 2 success (RV-CODE-006 PR #44 with old config posting `Failed to generate code suggestions for PR`; PR #48 with new config completing silently on markdown-only diff), 8 skipped (all matching `ignore_pr_title` filters). OmniRoute auth path working (`OPENAI_KEY: ${{ secrets.OMNIROUTE_API_KEY }}` + `api_base` override); `extra_instructions` parsed without error; trigger expansion (`synchronize`, `pull_request_review_comment`, `workflow_dispatch`) firing correctly. **Next code-PR will produce visible `/review` + `/improve` output** — no further config tuning expected for the pilot.

14. **Knowledge artifacts maintained.** `docs/backlog/food-lookup-followups.md` (BACKLOG-002@0.1.0) created with TKT-NEW-E/F/G entries; format mirrors BACKLOG-001@0.1.0 (post-TKT-005). `docs/session-log/` updated with this auto-cold snapshot (`2026-04-30-session-3.md`).

### 6.4 Next step (for you, new orchestrator)

Current v0.1 cycle is **between Tickets**. TKT-006 is closed; nothing is `in_progress` or `in_review`. The PO will choose the next ticket to dispatch from `ready` Tickets. No actions are blocking on the orchestrator at this snapshot. When the PO is ready to start the next cycle:

1. **PO selects a `ready` Ticket.** Most-likely candidates (status `ready` on `main`, no upstream blockers):
   - **TKT-007** Telegram inbound text → meal logging (depends on TKT-006@0.1.0 ✅ now done)
   - **TKT-008** Telegram voice → text (Whisper)
   - **TKT-009** Daily summary cron (depends on TKT-006@0.1.0 ✅)
   - **TKT-011** Goal-progress weekly digest (depends on TKT-006@0.1.0 ✅)
   - **TKT-014** Pilot KPI smoke suite (depends on TKT-006@0.1.0 ✅)
   - **TKT-015** Observability hardening (independent of all above; could run in parallel)
   - Verify the dependency graph by reading each Ticket's `depends_on:` frontmatter list.

2. **Dispatch fresh Executor session.** New Executor session per Ticket per `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §3. Default model GLM 5.1. Write paste-ready bootstrap at `~/handoff/EXECUTOR_BOOTSTRAP_TKT-NNN.md` mirroring `~/handoff/EXECUTOR_BOOTSTRAP_TKT-006.md` precedent. Required content:
   - Reference: `docs/prompts/executor.md` (system prompt to paste first into session).
   - Ticket inputs at version pins (e.g. `ARCH-001@0.4.0` §X.Y, `ADR-NNN@A.B.C`).
   - Question Protocol (Q-TKT-<NNN>-<NN> if spec lacks decisive answer).
   - Hard scope boundaries (Ticket §5 Outputs only).
   - PR opening contract.
   - **Branch hygiene:** verify `git merge-base origin/main HEAD == <fresh-main-sha>` before pushing — see PR #42 footgun in §6.2.

3. **Watch PR-Agent in action.** First non-clerical PR will produce visible PR-Agent output:
   - `/review` block (single comment, `persistent_comment=true`): security/effort/can-be-split/ticket-analysis labels, score, top findings.
   - Inline `/improve` suggestions: each commitable from GitHub UI (`commitable_code_suggestions=true`).
   - AI-walkthrough in PR description (only if PR template includes `<!-- description -->` markers).
   - Triggers: `opened`, `synchronize` (auto-rerun on iter-N pushes), `pull_request_review_comment`, manual `workflow_dispatch`.

4. **Dispatch fresh Reviewer session (Kimi K2.6) after Executor PR opens.** Same paste-ready bootstrap pattern (`~/handoff/REVIEWER_BOOTSTRAP_RV-CODE-NNN.md`). New Kimi session per artifact (different RV-CODE = different session). Reviewer iterates in the SAME session for iter-N (continuity). PR-Agent runs alongside but is informational — Kimi's verdict is load-bearing.

5. **Closure-PR pattern.** After Executor PR + Reviewer PR both merge to main, open clerical closure-PR with frontmatter flips + optional new BACKLOG-NNN file for deferred lows. Precedents: PR #36 (TKT-005), PR #43 (ADR-010), PR #48 (TKT-006).

6. **Auto-cold rule.** After every closed TKT cycle, generate next session-log snapshot (`<YYYY-MM-DD>-session-N.md`) without waiting for PO request, per `docs/session-log/README.md` §Auto-cold rule. Open as standard PR `session-log: auto-cold after <TKT-NNN> cycle close`.

### 6.5 Outstanding decisions / Q-PO items

- **Next-Ticket selection** (TKT-007 / TKT-008 / TKT-009 / TKT-011 / TKT-014 / TKT-015) — PO must choose. See §6.4 step 1.
- **TKT-NEW-D promotion** (calorie-floor clamp implementation per ADR-010): the ADR is `accepted` on main but no Executor TKT exists yet. Promotion is BACKLOG-001@0.1.0 §TKT-NEW-D → `docs/tickets/TKT-016-...md` (or next available number). PO bandwidth-pending.
- **TKT-NEW-A/B/C promotion** (carried from session-1 §6.5): still open; PO must greenlight each.
- **TKT-NEW-E/F/G promotion** (BACKLOG-002@0.1.0, NEW from PR #48): F-L1 dead `isPromptOrResponseSafeForLogging`, F-L2 deep-freeze `MANUAL_ENTRY_FAILURE_RESULT`, F-L3 decouple `LLM_TIMEOUT_MS`. All XS-sized; could batch into one TKT or run as solo XS tickets. PO bandwidth-pending.
- **F-L3 RV-SPEC-004 deferral** (carried from session-1 §6.5): still open.
- **Hidden findings on PR #41** — Devin Review panel showed «View 2 additional findings» but PO's ACUs were exhausted before they could be enumerated. RV-SPEC-005 `approved_note` documents the deferral. Devin Review is now deprecated (PR #47); these findings won't be retrievable from the bot. If any prove substantive in retrospective triage, route to a separate clerical follow-up PR.
- **Coder Plus model evaluation** (carried from §6.3 step 10): if `qwen3p6-coder-plus` becomes available on OmniRoute, swap is a one-line change in `.pr_agent.toml` (`config.model`). Marginal expected uplift on code-review tasks vs current `qwen3p6-plus` (~5-10% on benchmarks); not urgent.

### 6.6 Tooling assumptions

- Architect: GPT-5.5 xhigh in opencode CLI on PO's VPS (primary; per ratified `docs/prompts/architect.md` post-PR #39 cleanup). Alternatives: Opus 4.7 thinking Windsurf / Opus 4.6 thinking opencode.
- Executor: GLM 5.1 in opencode + OmniRoute on PO's VPS. Same opencode-session is reused across iterations of one ticket; new session per ticket.
- Reviewer (LLM): Kimi K2.6 in opencode + OmniRoute on PO's VPS. Same Kimi-session is reused across iterations of one review; new session per artifact.
- Reviewer (auto bot): Qodo PR-Agent (Qwen 3.6 Plus via OmniRoute / Fireworks). Heavy-tuned via PR #47. Auto-runs on every non-clerical PR. **Replaces Devin Review** (deprecated 2026-04-30 due to ACU exhaustion).
- VPS: 6 vCPU / 7.6 GiB RAM / 75 GB / Ubuntu 24.04. Repo path on VPS: `~/repos/openclown-assistant`.
- Devin native git tools (`git_view_pr` / `git_create_pr` / `git_pr_checks` / `git_ci_job_logs`) work via the org's git-manager proxy; `gh` CLI fallback is rarely needed for orchestrator work.
- OmniRoute endpoint: `https://omniroute.infinitycore.space:8443/v1`. Auth via `OMNIROUTE_API_KEY` GitHub Actions secret (used by PR-Agent workflow as `OPENAI_KEY`). Same endpoint serves Kimi K2.6 + GLM 5.1 + Qwen 3.6 Plus (via Fireworks model routing).
- npm quirk: `npm run lint` and `npm run typecheck` both alias to `tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json`. There is no ESLint pass in this repo as of 2026-04-30. If a future Ticket requires real linting, surface as a context-finding for orchestrator routing rather than treating as Executor defect.

### 6.7 Recent in-flight handoff files (`~/handoff/`)

The outgoing session produced several paste-ready files for Executor / Reviewer dispatches and rebases. They are NOT canonical and may be wiped on box restart — they served their purpose for the closed cycles. **The pattern is reusable for the next Ticket cycle:**

| Filename | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `~/handoff/EXECUTOR_BOOTSTRAP_TKT-006.md` | Initial Executor bootstrap (fresh GLM session, role prompt + ticket pins + scope boundaries + PR opening contract) | Used in session-2 — produced PR #42 |
| `~/handoff/EXECUTOR_NUDGE_iter-2_TKT-006.md` | Executor NUDGE iter-2 (existing GLM session: fix F-M1/F-M2/F-M3 with concrete tests, push to same branch) | Used — produced commit `08cec21` |
| `~/handoff/EXECUTOR_REBASE_PR42.md` | Executor REBASE NUDGE (existing GLM session: `git rebase --onto origin/main 2cefb7d` + force-push-with-lease) | Used — produced commit `528fa42` |
| `~/handoff/REVIEWER_BOOTSTRAP_RV-CODE-006.md` | Initial Reviewer bootstrap (fresh Kimi K2.6 session, §B CODE WORKFLOW with ticket pins + PR target + review scope guidance) | Used — produced PR #44 |
| `~/handoff/REVIEWER_NUDGE_iter-2_RV-CODE-006.md` | Reviewer NUDGE iter-2 (existing Kimi session: re-eval iter-2 commit, append-only iter-2 block, flip verdict if all M RESOLVED) | Used — produced commit `754d87b`, verdict pass |
| `~/handoff/REVIEWER_NUDGE_iter-2_RV-SPEC-005.md` | Reviewer NUDGE iter-2 for SPEC review (existing Kimi session, with `git rebase` workaround for `target_ref` footgun, since fixed by PR #46) | Used in session-2 — produced verdict pass on ADR-010 |
| `~/handoff/BACKLOG-002-food-lookup-followups.md` | Pre-staged BACKLOG-002 draft (TKT-NEW-E/F/G entries) | Used — became `docs/backlog/food-lookup-followups.md` in PR #48 |

**Pattern for next-Ticket dispatch.** The bootstrap / NUDGE pattern is documented in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §3 + the precedents above. New orchestrator should write fresh files for the next Ticket; reuse of these specific files is not appropriate (they are TKT-006 / RV-CODE-006 / RV-SPEC-005 specific).

---

## 7. After self-checks pass + required reading done

Reply to the PO with:

1. **5-line state summary** in your own words (proves you read this file + the current ArchSpec + recent reviews + open ticket pool + PR-Agent integration state)
2. **Role-confirmation:** "I am the orchestrator. I will not write code, will not edit PRDs/ArchSpecs/ADRs, and will only modify `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (lightly), and ticket / RV / ADR frontmatter promotion fields. Other CONTRIBUTING.md / `docs/prompts/` / `scripts/` / `.pr_agent.toml` / `.github/workflows/` edits require explicit PO authorisation, recorded in the PR body."
3. **One concrete proposed next action** with reasoning ("do X *because* Y trade-off"), citing the artifact / ADR / ticket that motivates it. The default obvious next action is §6.4 step 1 (next-Ticket selection from the `ready` pool).
4. **Wait for the PO to say "go"** before executing.

---

## 8. If something contradicts

If this file disagrees with `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` or with `CONTRIBUTING.md` — **the repo files win, this snapshot loses**. Snapshots are volatile by definition; the repo is the source of truth across handoffs. Tell the PO what you found.
